title: Skopeo exports to ctr imports compatibility
body:
I'm trying to switch from using `docker save` to `skopeo copy` for exporting Docker images when building offline installer.
When attempting to import archives created via Skopeo as `oci-archive` or `docker-archive` on new host, the image is not imported into the system and crt (containerd) returns an empty response.
However, there are no issues with archives created through docker save.

When comparing the contents of the archives, I noticed they have different structures and contents.
The manifest.json files in the archives created with docker and Skopeo have different structures.

Example of archives that can be successfully imported back via crt or docker:
```sh
$ docker save -o /tmp/doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar docker.io/rancher/mirrored-pause:3.6
$ tar -tf /tmp/doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
blobs/sha256/1021ef88c7974bfff89c5a0ec4fd3160daac6c48a075f74cff721f85dd104e68
blobs/sha256/16974531848218d24822bf606be022d030ab8c9b05b2ecf11076c4c1c6885c95
blobs/sha256/6270bb605e12e581514ada5fd5b3216f727db55dc87d5889c790e4c760683fee
blobs/sha256/bade096c2bdf1299856ac637d5a025dc30f275d3f897e1610666dc085174f030
index.json
manifest.json
oci-layout
repositories

$ sudo ctr -n k8s.io images export /tmp/ctr_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar docker.io/rancher/mirrored-pause:3.6
$ tar -tf /tmp/ctr_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
blobs/
blobs/sha256/
blobs/sha256/1021ef88c7974bfff89c5a0ec4fd3160daac6c48a075f74cff721f85dd104e68
blobs/sha256/16974531848218d24822bf606be022d030ab8c9b05b2ecf11076c4c1c6885c95
blobs/sha256/6270bb605e12e581514ada5fd5b3216f727db55dc87d5889c790e4c760683fee
index.json
manifest.json
oci-layout
```


These archives could not be successfully import via crt:
```sh
$ skopeo copy docker://docker.io/rancher/mirrored-pause:3.6 oci-archive:/tmp/sko_oci_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
$ tar -tf /tmp/sko_oci_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
blobs/sha256/253878249d7aef127ed0e137781059d2973f4037d9385de3b87ea82d224c3089
blobs/sha256/a9840e96a03be75d1dabca7c782f35e77b2e9834f8341c1dcc65c528ed03b9e9
blobs/sha256/fbe1a72f5dcd08ba4ca3ce3468c742786c1f6578c1f6bb401be1c4620d6ff705
index.json
oci-layout

$ skopeo copy docker://docker.io/rancher/mirrored-pause:3.6 docker-archive:/tmp/sko_doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
$ tar -tf /tmp/sko_doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
1021ef88c7974bfff89c5a0ec4fd3160daac6c48a075f74cff721f85dd104e68.tar
6270bb605e12e581514ada5fd5b3216f727db55dc87d5889c790e4c760683fee.json
6661348bfbea49561fef7f77d36ad354c45908c8c11aa7d87b0ab3472495e5b8/layer.tar
6661348bfbea49561fef7f77d36ad354c45908c8c11aa7d87b0ab3472495e5b8/VERSION
6661348bfbea49561fef7f77d36ad354c45908c8c11aa7d87b0ab3472495e5b8/json
manifest.json
repositories
```


I tested this on containerd v1 and v2. Here is an example of importing archives:
```sh
$ ctr -v
ctr containerd.io 1.7.23
$ bin/ctr -v
ctr github.com/containerd/containerd/v2 v2.0.4

$ sudo ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
unpacking docker.io/rancher/mirrored-pause:3.6 (sha256:16974531848218d24822bf606be022d030ab8c9b05b2ecf11076c4c1c6885c95)...done

$ sudo bin/ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
docker.io/rancher/mirrored pause:3.6    	saved
Importing	elapsed: 0.1 s	total:   0.0 B	(0.0 B/s)

$ sudo ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/sko_oci_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
$ sudo bin/ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/sko_oci_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar

$ sudo ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/sko_doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
$ sudo bin/ctr -n k8s.io images import /tmp/sko_doc_docker.io_rancher_mirrored-pause_3.6.tar
```

It is not possible to use Skopeo for importing on end systems, as they cannot install Skopeo. Is there a way to export a Docker image using Skopeo that would have a structure similar to those created by docker save or ctr images export?

comments.body:
Thanks for reaching out.

What is the actual failure here? The imports seem to have _succeeded_ per the above transcript.

Sure, the archives look different, but they have somewhat different goals, and without identifying the relevant part, “make the archives exactly the same as that other tool” is not an obvious improvement.

Guessing, might it be the case that `ctr` expects the archives to contain tagged images? Writing to `docker-archive:$path` or `oci-archive:$path` stores the image, but does not automatically add any name (the source and destination parameters of `skopeo copy` are independent and don’t heuristically inherit anything in either direction). Consider `{docker,oci}-archive:$path:$tag`.

Hi, thank you for your response.

After executing the import command, no error occurs, but nothing gets loaded as a result.
Yes, I understand — I meant it more in terms of compatibility, to ensure the import is possible.
I’ll try comparing the manifests.

I compared manifest.json files from archives created using docker save and skopeo copy with docker-archive format. It turns out that when generating manifest.json for the docker-archive type, the RepoTags field must be populated.

I replaced `"RepoTags": [],` with `"RepoTags": ["quay.io/curl/curl:latest"],` and ctr images import successfully imported the archive.

How to reproduce this issue:
```shell
$ skopeo copy -q docker://quay.io/curl/curl:latest docker-archive:/tmp/test/skopeo_docker_curl.tar

$ sudo ctr -n moby images import /tmp/test/skopeo_docker_curl.tar
$ sudo ctr -n moby images ls
REF TYPE DIGEST SIZE PLATFORMS LABELS

$ mkdir -p /tmp/extract_skopeo && tar -xf /tmp/test/skopeo_docker_curl.tar -C /tmp/extract_skopeo
$ cat /tmp/extract_skopeo/manifest.json | jq | grep RepoTags
    "RepoTags": [],
```

Example of docker save and import:
```shell
$ docker save -o /tmp/test/docker.tar quay.io/curl/curl:latest

$ sudo ctr -n moby images import /tmp/test/docker.tar
unpacking quay.io/curl/curl:latest (sha256:6ad4af5b0bcaec67c22e617efaa330fd2f9518dbdc8b6fc7ad1351d183358b1a)...done
$ sudo ctr -n moby images ls
REF                      TYPE                                       DIGEST                                                                  SIZE     PLATFORMS   LABELS
quay.io/curl/curl:latest application/vnd.oci.image.manifest.v1+json sha256:6ad4af5b0bcaec67c22e617efaa330fd2f9518dbdc8b6fc7ad1351d183358b1a 21.3 MiB linux/amd64 -

$ mkdir -p /tmp/extract_docker && tar -xf /tmp/test/docker.tar -C /tmp/extract_docker
$ cat /tmp/extract_docker/manifest.json | jq | grep RepoTags -A 2
    "RepoTags": [
      "quay.io/curl/curl:latest"
    ],
```

Repack of docker-archive based archive to fill RepoTags: 
```shell
$ mkdir -p /tmp/extract_skopeo && tar -xf /tmp/test/skopeo_docker_curl.tar -C /tmp/extract_skopeo
$ cat /tmp/extract_skopeo/manifest.json | jq | grep RepoTags
    "RepoTags": [],

$ sed -i 's/"RepoTags":\[\]/"RepoTags":\["quay.io\/curl\/curl:latest"\]/g' /tmp/extract_skopeo/manifest.json
$ cat /tmp/extract_skopeo/manifest.json | jq | grep -A2 RepoTags
    "RepoTags": [
      "quay.io/curl/curl:latest"
    ],
$ tar -cf /tmp/test/skopeo_docker_curl_updated.tar -C /tmp/extract_skopeo .

$ sudo ctr -n moby images import /tmp/test/skopeo_docker_curl_updated.tar
unpacking quay.io/curl/curl:latest (sha256:97fb88d49b8853df903536647580a946d0c53fcb79a7ecf6c1f8574ca65298ad)...done
$ sudo ctr -n moby images ls
REF                      TYPE                                                 DIGEST                                                                  SIZE     PLATFORMS   LABELS
quay.io/curl/curl:latest application/vnd.docker.distribution.manifest.v2+json sha256:97fb88d49b8853df903536647580a946d0c53fcb79a7ecf6c1f8574ca65298ad 21.3 MiB linux/amd64 -
$ sudo ctr -n moby images rm quay.io/curl/curl:latest
quay.io/curl/curl:latest
```
Yes; I have already suggested including a tag in the destination option to `skopeo copy`.

Hi, yes, additional-tag works. 

```bash
$ skopeo copy --additional-tag quay.io/curl/curl:latest docker://quay.io/curl/curl:latest docker-archive:/tmp/skopeo_docker_curl.tar

$ sudo ctr -n moby images import /tmp/skopeo_docker_curl.tar
unpacking quay.io/curl/curl:latest (sha256:97fb88d49b8853df903536647580a946d0c53fcb79a7ecf6c1f8574ca65298ad)...done
```

Maybe we should fill in this tag by default to make imports via ctr work smoothly without needing extra steps.
Since Docker does this automatically when exporting.

`--additional-tag` works but it is not the most straightforward use:

> Consider `{docker,oci}-archive:$path:$tag`.

`skopeo copy` is a general, fairly low-level, tool; it _intentionally_ does not contain any heuristics to inherit/propagate names between sources and destinations. (If we did inherit, would we then need a `copy --no-default-{docker,oci}-tag` option??) .

`skopeo copy` can, of course, be used as the underlying tool in a higher-level script.
